escalation.rationale: US airstrikes entered their sixth consecutive night targeting Iranian infrastructure including Chabahar port and bridges in Hormozgan province, while Iran struck US Navy facilities in Bahrain and threatened full regional oil export blockade, with Houthis placed on standby to close Bab al-Mandeb.

ticker_items: US collapses tower at Iran's Chabahar port in sixth night of strikes.
Iran IRGC claims strike on US Navy facilities in Bahrain.
Iran asks Houthis to prepare Bab al-Mandeb closure as oil prices surge.
US Senate advances sweeping Russia sanctions bill targeting China and India.
Russia drops five guided bombs on Ukraine's Sumy, hitting civilian infrastructure.

new_entrant.text: Houthi forces in Yemen are being activated by Iran as a potential second front, with orders to prepare drone and missile strikes to close Bab al-Mandeb strait, effectively entering the US-Iran conflict as a proxy belligerent.

eastern_europe.description: Russia dropped five guided bombs on Sumy, striking civilian infrastructure.

middle_east.description: US airstrikes collapsed a surveillance tower at Iran's Chabahar port on the Gulf of Oman, a key trade route for Afghanistan, confirmed by US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
US airstrikes hit and destroyed multiple bridges overnight in Iran's southern Hormozgan province, killing at least seven people according to Iranian state television.
Iran's IRGC claimed it struck US Navy facilities in Bahrain and warned of a full regional oil and gas export blockade.

today.incident: US strikes on Iran - cumulative Iranian claims
Russian guided bomb strike on Sumy

Killed in Gulf states (US-Israel campaign, cumulative)
Russian troops surviving average battlefield tenure

ukraine.text: Ukraine will build 5 million drones in 2026; NATO must learn how.
ukraine.speaker: Johnny Stringer
ukraine.title: NATO Deputy Commander, Air Chief Marshal

middle_east.text: We are winning big in Iran.
middle_east.speaker: Donald Trump
middle_east.title: US President

middle_east.text: Regional oil and gas exports could be blocked entirely in response to American attempts to control the Strait of Hormuz.
middle_east.speaker: IRGC spokesperson
middle_east.title: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps

middle_east.text: Israel is determined to keep its forces in security zones it has carved out inside Lebanon, Syria and the Gaza Strip.
middle_east.speaker: Israel Katz
middle_east.title: Israeli Defence Minister

observers.description: The US is conducting its sixth consecutive night of airstrikes against Iranian infrastructure including ports and bridges in Hormozgan province. US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth publicly confirmed the Chabahar port strike. The Senate is also advancing sweeping Russia sanctions legislation with White House backing.

Iran's IRGC claimed strikes on US Navy facilities in Bahrain and has reportedly instructed Houthi allies to prepare to close Bab al-Mandeb. Iran has resumed its Strait of Hormuz blockade, significantly reducing shipping traffic, and warned of full regional oil export closure.

Israel confirmed to US Defense Secretary Hegseth that it intends to maintain troops in security zones inside Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza. Former NSA Meir Ben-Shabbat warned against retreats in Lebanon amid the broader US-Iran conflict. Over half of House Democrats revolted against a $3.3 billion aid package to Israel.

Iran has reportedly instructed its Houthi allies to prepare drones and missiles for potential closure of the Bab al-Mandeb strait. This would add a second major maritime chokepoint to the conflict. Houthi activation would significantly escalate the regional energy supply crisis.

Russia conducted guided bomb strikes on Sumy targeting civilian infrastructure. A bipartisan US Senate bill proposes 100% tariffs on the top buyers of Russian oil including China and India, which has White House backing. Russia is also offering India joint production of next-generation T-90MS tanks.

China faces potential US secondary sanctions as one of the largest buyers of Russian oil under the advancing Senate bill. Xi Jinping recently visited North Korea's Kim Jong Un, pledging closer diplomatic, law enforcement, and military cooperation. China-North Korea alignment is deepening amid geopolitical fragmentation.

Kim Jong Un met with a top Chinese official following Xi's visit, with both sides pledging closer military cooperation. North Korea's activities near the DMZ are raising concerns in South Korea about potential armistice violations. Pyongyang is exploiting geopolitical fragmentation to evade nuclear sanctions.

India is named as one of the top five buyers of Russian oil potentially targeted by the US Senate sanctions bill, creating significant diplomatic pressure on New Delhi. Russia is simultaneously offering India joint T-90MS tank production. India is navigating competing pressures from Washington and Moscow.

Zelensky's dismissal of Defense Minister Fedorov has triggered protest rallies across Ukraine and concerns about defense leadership stability. Lockheed Martin has signaled support for Ukraine producing PAC-3 interceptors domestically. Ukraine is on pace to build 5 million drones in 2026, with AI-powered systems inflicting severe Russian casualties.

financial.notes: Oil prices rose sharply on Friday as intensifying US-Iran hostilities and Iran's threat to close both Strait of Hormuz and Bab al-Mandeb stoked supply disruption fears, with Rystad Energy revising its US-Iran scenario framework citing renewed Hormuz security deterioration.

update_log.text: US airstrikes collapse surveillance tower at Iran's Chabahar port on Gulf of Oman in sixth consecutive night of attacks; confirmed by US SecDef Hegseth.
US strikes destroy multiple bridges in Iran's Hormozgan province overnight, killing at least seven civilians per Iranian state media.
Iran's IRGC claims strike on US Navy facilities in Bahrain and threatens total blockade of regional oil and gas exports.
Iran reportedly instructs Houthi forces in Yemen to prepare drone and missile capabilities to close Bab al-Mandeb strait.
Russia drops five guided bombs on Sumy, Ukraine, striking civilian infrastructure.
US Senate bipartisan bill proposing 100% tariffs on top Russian oil buyers including China and India advances with White House backing.
North Korean leader Kim Jong Un meets top Chinese official following Xi's visit pledging closer military and diplomatic cooperation.
Israel's Defense Minister tells US SecDef Hegseth that Israel will maintain forces in security zones in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza.
CIA Director Ratcliffe states Russian troops survive an average of 30 minutes on the Ukrainian battlefield due to AI-powered drone attacks.
